title: Mimph
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Species |image=[[File:Mimph-GWtM.png]] |name=Mimph |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Semi-sentient]]{{CSWECite|II|315|Mimph}} |class= |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor= |haircolor=[[Color/Legends|Pink]], white{{Ewoks|Gone With the Mimphs}} |feathers= |eyecolor=White; black [[Eye/Legends|pupils]] |distinctions=Small stature; long [[Ear/Legends|ears]] and whiskers |lifespan= |origin=[[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]] |habitat= |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|Give up! You're a prisoner of the mimphs!|Masher|}} '''Mimphs''' were small, [[Sentience/Legends|semi-sentient]] creatures native to the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. Although not fully sentient, mimphs were intelligent, and they were able to comprehend and use [[Language/Legends|language]] to communicate with fully sentient beings like [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]]. They were also able to manipulate tools and build [[City/Legends|villages]], and teams of mimphs could even capture much larger prey. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], a group of mimphs from a [[Mimph village|community]] near [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]] captured the Ewok [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]] and put him on display in their village. When a monstrous [[hanadak]] rampaged through the village, Warrick defeated the beast and saved the village residents. Later, when Endor was [[Winter/Legends|covered]] in snow and [[Water/Legends|ice]] by the [[Snow King]], the village was buried, and the mimphs had to use shovels to remove the massive amounts of snow until the Snow [[King/Legends|King]] was [[Skirmish at the Snow Palace|defeated]]. ==Biology and appearance== {{Quote|Captured by a bunch of runts. If anyone sees me like this, I'll ''never'' live it down!|Wicket Wystri Warrick|}} Mimphs were tiny, [[Sentience/Legends|semi-sentient]] creatures which, as adults, stood about ankle-high to an [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]]. A mimph walked on two [[Limb/Legends|legs]] and had two three-fingered hands; two long, forked [[Ear/Legends|ears]]; a flat, [[Heart/Legends|heart]]-shaped tail; and a [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] full of [[Color/Legends|white]] [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]]. Mostly covered in pink fur, the lower half of a mimph's face was white, as was the inside of its ears. Their two [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] were mostly white, with black pupils and pink lids, and were situated below two eyebrows, which could be white or black. Two whiskers protruded from both sides of the small, red [[Nose/Legends|nose]]. While their limbs were quite thin compared to their bodies, their heads were large—about the size of the torso. Some mimphs were known to grow white facial [[Hair/Legends|hair]]. To Ewoks, mimphs seemed to have high-pitched voices. ==Society and culture== {{Quote|We're taking you back to our village!''"
"''Yeah, our village!|Two mimphs|}} [[File:MimphVillage-GWtM.png|thumb|left|A mimph village]] Mimphs lived in [[City/Legends|villages]], which contained houses constructed from firm [[Plant/Legends|plant]] material around blades of tall, [[Typha|thick grass]], to which the material was tied at the top and bottom. Many mimph homes were brown with green roofs and balconies; a single round hole allowed access to the interior. Larger structures with similar color schemes and layouts were often constructed atop [[Tree/Legends|tree]] trunks. Like their homes, mimph clothing was often similar; a standard design consisted of a tan body suit with feet, decorated by a horizontal yellow stripe across the chest. Some also had a red [[Diamond/Legends|diamond]] centered on the [[Stomach/Legends|stomach]]. However, mimphs had several different types of [[Hat/Legends|headgear]]; variants included a horned [[Armor/Legends|helmet]] worn by [[Masher]]. Despite their small stature in comparison to many other [[Species/Legends|species]], mimphs were fierce [[Hunting/Legends|hunters]], frequently banding together in parties to capture larger prey. Their limited mental capacity did not inhibit their ability to outwit sentient opponents, with whom they could communicate if they spoke a mutual [[Language/Legends|language]], and they mastered the art of hunting by using lengths of [[Rope/Legends|rope]] and large, wheeled vehicles to more easily transport their prizes. Mimph [[Youngling/Legends|children]] were cared for by their [[Parent/Legends|mothers]], and many mimphs were known to own and use [[Water/Legends|snow]] shovels, which they used to clean their villages in the [[Winter/Legends|winter]]. Fascinated by larger creatures, mimphs were known to put those that they captured on display for the general public's entertainment. ==History== ===Early history and Wicket's capture=== {{Quote|Please, take this gift.''"
"''What is it?''"
"''A mimph tooth for your belt of honor! Thrasher lost it in a fight.|Masher and Wicket|}} The diminutive mimphs evolved on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]], a [[Moon/Legends|moon]] with a wide variety of terrains, where they constructed villages and hunted for larger prey. By [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=222689&start=1905#14621041|thread=Books, Comics, & Television VIPs|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-04-20|time=11:42 AM|quote=The 3 ABY dating (technically, it should be 3.5) is from the timeline in Behind the Magic.}} [[Mimph village|one such village]] had been founded near a [[Unidentified Endor river (mimph village)|river]] not far from the Ewoks of [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]]. [[File:MimphsWicket-GWtM.png|thumb|right|The mimphs put Wicket on display.]] In that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], the village was the home of the mimphs [[Smasher]], [[Thrasher (mimph)|Thrasher]], and [[Masher]]. These three mimphs, as well as [[Unidentified mimph|one other]], found the young Ewok [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]] unconscious on the banks of the nearby river. Warrick, who had been battling a fearsome, gargantuan beast called a [[hanadak]] in an attempt to acquire a [[Tooth/Legends|fang]] for his [[Belt of Honor]], awoke to the four mimphs standing on top of him. Tied to a wheeled vehicle with the mimphs' rope, Warrick was unable to escape, and the mimphs declared that they would take him back to their village. There, the mimphs found Warrick a giant in comparison to themselves. The immense Ewok was dangerous too; a simple sneeze by the outsider was enough to destroy several of the village's tiny buildings. The mimph leaders kept the Ewok tied up behind a curtain that overlooked the settlement. As the residents cheered and shouted to see the Ewok, Masher introduced Warrick as "the monstrous; the fearsome: Wicket!" The curtain was drawn back, and the entire village stared at the giant Ewok in horror. As the crowd gazed, mimph mothers shielded their children's eyes, and Masher asked Warrick to put on a show by roaring. The Ewok, however, refused, and instead mumbled the expletive "[[Ewokese/Legends|kvark]]" to himself. As the mimphs cheered on, repeating the word, the hanadak heard the commotion and entered the village. The beast tore through the area and destroyed buildings with its feet and hands. The four mimphs who had captured Warrick leaped onto the hanadak's leg in a futile attempt to slow the beast down. Warrick, concerned for the mimphs' safety, tore free of his bonds. The hanadak continued its rampage, swatting the four mimphs into the [[Atmosphere/Legends|air]], but they were caught by Warrick, who set them down gently and grabbed the curtain that the mimphs had used to hide him. After climbing a tall tree, Warrick leaped onto the hanadak's shoulders and, using the curtain as a blindfold, led the hanadak toward the river where the mimphs had kidnapped him. Warrick leaped to safety as the hanadak fell in to the water and was dropped over a waterfall. The Ewok then expressed to the mimphs his disappointment in having failed to acquire a hanadak fang for his Belt of Honor. The four mimphs, grateful for the Ewok's help in defeating the creature,{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}} considered the matter and granted the Ewok a tooth from Thrasher, who had lost it in a fight. ===Other crises=== {{Quote|We're not the only ones who have it rough. Look what's happened to the poor little mimph village!|Wicket|{{Ewoks|The Season Scepter}}}} [[File:MimphSnowDay-TSS.png|thumb|left|The mimph village, covered in snow]] Later in 3 ABY, the weather on Endor turned unusually cold and snowy in the middle of the [[Summer/Legends|summer]] season, and the mimph village was buried in snow. The mimphs were forced to use shovels to remove the powder, which reached a depth equal to a mimph's stature in some places. As the Ewok [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]], who had recently [[The Force/Legends|cast]] a weather-altering spell, believed he was to blame for the snow, he and his friends Warrick, [[Latara/Legends|Latara]], and the [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]] journeyed to the [[Sun King's palace]], home of the powerful being known as the [[Sun King]], to explain the situation. On their way to the [[palace]], the Ewoks witnessed the mimphs clearing their village of the snow, a spectacle that added to Teebo's feelings of guilt. When the Ewoks learned that the magical artifact known as the [[season scepter]] had been [[Robbery at the Sun Palace|stolen]] by the Sun [[King/Legends|King's]] brother, the [[Snow King]], the Ewoks journeyed to to the [[Snow Palace]], where they [[Skirmish at the Snow Palace|defeated]] the Snow King and convinced him to return Endor to its summer season. The harsh and unnatural winter ended, and all of Endor, including the mimph village, was returned to the summer. On another occasion that year, a group of mimphs came under attack by a [[Unidentified green-tongued creature|hairy, bipedal creature]]. Just as the beast threatened to hurl two houses-ful of mimphs into a yawning maw, the Ewoks Wicket W. Warrick and Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka swooped down on a [[Hang-glider/Legends|skin glider]] and swiped the mimphs from the [[Diet/Legends|predator's]] grasp.[[Ewoks: Season Two|''Ewoks'': Season Two]] opening credits ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Creature_attacks_mimphs_2.png|thumb|right|Two houses of mimphs come under attack by a creature during the opening credits of the second season of ''Ewoks''.]] The mimphs first appeared during the [[Ewoks: Season Two|second season]] of the [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] television series as a part of the show's opening credits animation sequence. They received their first starring role in the story of "[[Gone With the Mimphs]]," the ninth episode of the season. The episode was written by [[Linda Woolverton]] and first aired on [[October 18]], [[1986]]. Mimphs appeared briefly in the season's thirteenth episode, "[[The Season Scepter]]," which was written by [[Bob Carrau]] and aired on [[November 1]], 1986. Both episodes picture mimphs as having a dark-red covering on their heads, and also depict some as having a pair of horns. While neither episode makes it clear whether these coverings and horns are a form of headgear or part of the mimphs' natural anatomy, this article assumes the former. No source has clarified when the events of the opening credits take place; this article assumes they must occur after Wicket W. Warrick meets the mimphs in "Gone With the Mimphs," but the placement of the beast attack on the mimphs is otherwise arbitrary. The article "[[A Star Wars CELibration]]," which was published in the [[1995]] magazine [[Star Wars Insider 27|''Star Wars Insider'' 27]] and which provides plot summaries for all ''Star Wars: Ewoks'' and [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] episodes, capitalizes the M in "Mimph." However, the [[2008]] reference book ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', which establishes that the creatures are semi-sentient and native to Endor, does not. This article assumes that the more recent ''Encyclopedia'' is correct. ==Appearances== *{{Ewoks|Gone With the Mimphs}} *{{Ewoks|The Season Scepter}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of mimphs}} *[[Ewoks: Season Two|''Ewoks'': Season Two]] {{C|Opening credits}} {{1stm}} *{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{SW|url=series/clone-wars/nomad-droids-episode-guide|text="Nomad Droids" Episode Guide | The Clone Wars}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Mimph }} [[Category:Semi-sentient species]] [[Category:Sentient species of Endor]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Mimphs]] }}
